matter: In re Meridian Semiconductor Corp. Securities Litigation
section: Section VII: Damages from the March 12 Corrective Disclosure
text: Meridian's stock fell 18.4% on March 12, 2024, from $64.20 to $52.39 (Ex. 14). The decline was statistically significant at the 1% level per the event study (Ex. 14). Trading volume on March 12 was 6.2× the prior 90-day average (Ex. 15). No confounding company-specific news was released that day (Ex. 16). Analyst reaction attributed the decline to the restatement announcement (Ex. 17). Estimated aggregate damages are $412 million (Ex. 18). The S&P Semiconductor index declined 0.3% the same day (Ex. 14). Meridian's CFO had certified the prior financials under SOX §302 (Ex. 19). Institutional investors held 71% of the float during the class period (Ex. 20). Short interest was at a 52-week high in the week before disclosure (Ex. 16).
